2606.23361.title: Rethinking Molecular Graph Backdoors under Chemistry-aware Admission
2606.23361.paper_summary: Backdoor attacks on molecular graph neural networks (GNNs) are typically evaluated as abstract graph edits, but real molecular learning pipelines do not train on arbitrary graphs. Molecular records must first survive parsing, sanitization, canonicalization, and graph-string consistency checks. We formalize this overlooked admission stage as ChemGuard, an operational protocol for testing whether a submitted molecular record can enter a realistic learning pipeline, while complementing existing defenses. ChemGuard admits a record only when its molecular string is sanitizable and the graph reconstructed from that string matches the submitted molecular graph. Under this operational view, many existing graph-based backdoors lose much of their apparent efficacy because their poisons are chemically invalid or representation-inconsistent. We then show that admission checks alone are insufficient to rule out molecular backdoors. We propose ChemBack, an admission-aware molecular backdoor attack that constructs chemically feasible motif-anchor attachments and ranks admitted candidates by fingerprint-based Tanimoto similarity to clean target-class molecules. ChemBack is model-free during trigger selection, using molecular structures, target labels, fingerprints, and public validity checks, but no victim model, surrogate GNN, learned embedding, gradient, logit, or training-code access. Across molecular benchmarks, validators, architectures, and defenses, \textbf{ChemBack} achieves high attack success with fully admitted poisons while preserving clean accuracy. Our results reveal a two-sided lesson, chemistry-aware admission suppresses many graph-only backdoors, yet chemically valid and target-aligned molecular backdoors remain a practical threat.
2606.23361.paper_summary_zh: 對分子圖神經網絡（GNN）的後門攻擊通常被評估為抽象的圖編輯，但實際的分子學習管道並不會在任意圖上進行訓練。分子記錄必須首先經過解析、清理、標準化和圖-字符串一致性檢查。我們將這一被忽視的入場階段形式化為ChemGuard，一種操作協議，用於測試提交的分子記錄是否可以進入現實的學習管道，同時補充現有的防禦措施。ChemGuard僅在其分子字符串可清理且從該字符串重建的圖與提交的分子圖匹配時，才接受該記錄。在這一操作視角下，許多現有的基於圖的後門失去了其表面上的有效性，因為它們的毒素在化學上無效或表示不一致。我們接著展示，僅僅依賴入場檢查不足以排除分子後門。我們提出了ChemBack，一種考慮入場的分子後門攻擊，該攻擊構建化學上可行的基元-錨點附加物，並根據指紋基於Tanimoto相似度對已入場的候選者進行排名。ChemBack在觸發選擇過程中是無模型的，使用分子結構、目標標籤、指紋和公共有效性檢查，但不使用受害者模型、替代GNN、學習嵌入、梯度、邏輯或訓練代碼訪問。在分子基準、驗證者、架構和防禦中，\textbf{ChemBack}在完全入場的毒素下實現了高攻擊成功率，同時保持了清晰的準確性。我們的結果揭示了一個雙面教訓，化學感知的入場抑制了許多僅基於圖的後門，但化學上有效且目標對齊的分子後門仍然是一種實際威脅。
2606.23361.author: Thinh T. H. Nguyen et.al.
2606.23361.authors: Thinh T. H. Nguyen, Sze Jue Yang, Khoa D. Doan, Chee Seng Chan, Kok-Seng Wong
